response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend web developer with a broad polyglot background, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The two hardest blockers are absolute disqualifiers: the role requires native-level Japanese proficiency, and Bogdan lists no Japanese ability whatsoever; and the role demands deep embedded C/Linux-kernel security work, while Bogdan's career is almost entirely web-oriented (Node.js, TypeScript, Ember.js, MongoDB). His limited C/C++ exposure is over a decade old and unrelated to embedded networking or LAN-switch firmware. There is no evidence of experience with any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or Layer-2/3 networking, making him unsuitable for this position.
strengths: Long, continuous professional track record (15+ years) demonstrating adaptability across multiple stacks and problem domains
Some low-level and systems exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, high-performance async I/O work in DLang at Sociomantic, and a Linux-based wave-field synthesis thesis project
Strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and open-source contributions that indicate code quality discipline
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency at any level — this is a stated hard requirement and an absolute disqualifier
Zero experience with embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), or Linux kernel development
Primary skill set is web/cloud (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS) with no meaningful recent C or embedded systems work
